title: lichen-node TxError::BufferTooSmall should wrap lichen_core::error::BufferTooSmall
description:
lichen-node/src/stack.rs defines:
  pub enum TxError {
      CoapEncode,
      SchcCompress,
      FrameEncode,
      RadioTx,
      BufferTooSmall,  // unit variant
  }

This is the same pattern as the other error inconsistencies. BufferTooSmall is a unit variant without size information.

lichen-schc properly wraps the core type:
  pub enum SchcError {
      BufferTooSmall(BufferTooSmall),  // wraps with required/provided sizes
      ...
  }

What goes wrong: TX failures due to buffer size cannot report how large a buffer was needed. This makes debugging difficult since callers must guess the required size rather than being told exactly.
